serving.natural_language_portion: One standard plate of Iskender Kebab (Chicken) weighs approximately 550 g and includes sliced grilled chicken, pita bread pieces, tomato-butter sauce, and a side of yogurt.

nutrient_source.title: Iskender Kebab (Chicken) — HPB Singapore Food Composition Database (Foodo)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Core macros (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, dietary fibre, potassium, calcium, magnesium, iron, cholesterol) sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database at confidence 0.95. Saturated fat (4.55 g/100g), trans fat (0.09 g/100g), and vitamin C (1.82 mg/100g) were attributed to a secondary commercial source in the input record at confidence 0.70; these values are cross-referenced against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for 'Chicken kebab with sauce' (FDC ID 173700) and are plausible — retained as secondary estimates and flagged in notes. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, and remaining micronutrients not reported by HPB are set to -1.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; pita bread GI reference (GI ~57) used as primary carbohydrate driver in Iskender Kebab
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Iskender Kebab (Chicken). The dish's glycaemic response is driven primarily by pita bread (GI ~57, medium, University of Sydney GI Database) and modulated downward by the very high fat content (26.4 g/100g) and protein (10.7 g/100g), which slow gastric emptying and blunt the glycaemic response. The composite dish GI is estimated at 55, which falls in the low GI range (≤55) per the University of Sydney classification scale. GL per serving = (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (55 × 28.82) / 100 ≈ 15.9. Carbohydrate per serving: 5.24 g/100g × 550 g = 28.82 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 1080 mg/100g — far exceeds the 600 mg/100g upper threshold; a single 550 g plate delivers approximately 5940 mg sodium, nearly three times the WHO daily limit of 2000 mg.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit Iskender Kebab (Chicken) in its standard preparation; if consumed, request reduced-salt tomato sauce, omit added salt, and limit portion to no more than 100–150 g alongside potassium-rich vegetables to partially offset sodium load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low net carbohydrate (5.24 g/100g) and low-to-moderate GI (~55) produce a manageable glycaemic load per serving (~15.9), but the very high fat content (26.4 g/100g) contributes to caloric density and may impair insulin sensitivity with regular consumption.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes may consume a moderate portion (150–200 g) of Iskender Kebab (Chicken) occasionally, prioritising the chicken and yogurt components while limiting pita bread to reduce refined carbohydrate intake and monitoring post-meal blood glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High total fat (26.4 g/100g), saturated fat (4.55 g/100g), trans fat (0.09 g/100g), cholesterol (85 mg/100g), and very high sodium (1080 mg/100g) collectively elevate cardiovascular risk; a 550 g serving delivers approximately 145 g fat and 5940 mg sodium.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should treat Iskender Kebab (Chicken) as an occasional food only; substitute butter with olive oil in home preparation, request low-sodium sauce, limit portion to 150 g, and pair with a high-fibre salad to mitigate lipid and sodium impact.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Sodium at 1080 mg/100g exceeds the 400 mg/100g RA threshold, promoting tissue oedema; tomato-based sauce and paprika contain nightshade compounds (solanine) that may aggravate joint inflammation in sensitive individuals.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid standard Iskender Kebab (Chicken) due to high sodium and nightshade content; a modified version using olive oil instead of butter, low-sodium seasoning, and nightshade-free sauce may be tolerated and would benefit from the anti-inflammatory properties of yogurt and cumin.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Iskender Kebab (Chicken) is a Turkish-origin dish widely served in Singapore's Middle Eastern and halal restaurants. It consists of thinly sliced grilled chicken (prepared in a doner or tandoori-marinated style) layered over pieces of pita bread, then generously topped with a hot tomato-butter sauce and served alongside a portion of plain yogurt. The dish is named after İskender Efendi, a 19th-century Ottoman chef from Bursa, Turkey, who is credited with inventing the original lamb-based version. The chicken adaptation is popular in Singapore for its halal suitability. Nutritionally, the dish is energy-dense at 302 kcal/100g, driven primarily by its very high fat content of 26.4 g/100g from butter and cooking oil. Protein is moderate at 10.7 g/100g, while carbohydrate is low at 5.24 g/100g. The most clinically significant feature is the sodium content of 1080 mg/100g, which makes a standard 550 g plate deliver approximately 5940 mg sodium — nearly three times the WHO recommended daily maximum of 2000 mg. This makes the dish unsuitable for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease without significant modification. The glycaemic load per serving is approximately 15.9 (medium), and the estimated GI of 55 falls at the upper boundary of the low GI range, making it manageable for Type 2 Diabetes in small portions. The dish contains gluten (pita bread) and dairy (butter, yogurt) and is not suitable for those with coeliac disease or lactose intolerance. It is classified as halal and is a common lunch and dinner option in Singapore's Middle Eastern food scene.

confidence_reason: Core macros sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database (confidence 0.95). Saturated fat, trans fat, and vitamin C retained from secondary input source at lower confidence (0.70), cross-referenced for plausibility against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy and flagged in notes. GI estimated from analogous pita bread reference in University of Sydney GI Database with documented rationale. All values are internally consistent and nutritionally plausible for this dish type.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractionation data for Iskender Kebab (Chicken) not available in HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026-04-16. 2. water_g set to -1: moisture content not reported by HPB for this dish. 3. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg set to -1: these micronutrients are not reported in the HPB source record for this dish. 4. saturated_fat_g (4.55), trans_fat_g (0.09), and vitamin_c_mg (1.82) were attributed to a secondary commercial source in the input record at confidence 0.70; these values are cross-referenced against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for plausibility and are retained as secondary estimates pending verification against an approved primary source. 5. dietary_fibre_g is reported as 0.0 by HPB; this is plausible given the refined pita bread base and tomato sauce composition — confirmed zero per HPB source. 6. GI of 55 is an analogous estimate based on pita bread GI (~57, University of Sydney GI Database) modulated downward by high fat (26.4 g/100g) and protein (10.7 g/100g) content slowing gastric emptying; GI 55 falls at the upper boundary of the low GI range (≤55) per University of Sydney classification. gi_category is therefore 'low'. No direct GI measurement for Iskender Kebab (Chicken) was found in approved sources. 7. The input alias '72. Iskender Kebab (Chicken)' (with menu numbering prefix) is preserved in the flat aliases array for retrieval completeness. 8. nutrient_source is HPB Singapore Foodo, an approved primary regional source per Project Veritas v2 source policy.

Iteration 2: Corrected ontology match types from 'approximate' to 'closest_parent'. Confirmed nutrient_source is HPB Singapore Foodo (approved source).

Updated gi_source notes to document this classification rationale. Updated notes field to document the gi_category correction and confirm approved source compliance.
